summary: Quality assessment and comprehensive test strategy for src/lib/concerts.ts (25+ exported functions). Builds on established Vitest framework with GDPR-compliant test patterns, Prisma mocking, and 80% coverage target.

applicable: [H] All new service functions/methods have unit tests
[H] Tests cover: happy path, primary error paths, and key edge cases
[H] External dependencies (DB, external APIs) are mocked in unit tests
[M] Tests are independent — no shared mutable state between tests
[M] Integration tests exist for API endpoints (at minimum, smoke tests)
applicable_sections.details: All 7 items directly apply. CRITICAL: Coverage target is 80% which means > 20% of concerts.ts can remain untested. Focus test effort on hot spots: CRUD operations, pagination logic, statistics caching, and error paths.

applicable: [C] All external input is validated before use
[H] Input size limits are enforced: string max lengths, numeric bounds, array size limits
applicable_sections.details: concerts.ts accepts filters (userId, username, bandSlug, city, year, limit, offset, cursor). Tests MUST validate bounds (limit clamped 1-100) and type coercion.

applicable: [C] No unhandled exceptions can reach the client as a 500 with an internal stack trace
[C] All external calls (DB, API, file system) have explicit error handling
[H] Custom exception hierarchy is used for domain errors
applicable_sections.details: concerts.ts defines ConcertAlreadyExistsError custom exception. Tests MUST verify error is thrown and handled gracefully. All Prisma calls mocked.

applicable: [H] Structured logging is used: JSON in production, not console.log statements
[M] Service entry points log: what operation started, with what key identifiers
applicable_sections.details: concerts.ts currently has NO logging. Tests can skip observability testing for now (logging is a separate implementation task), but document this gap in risks section.

applicable: [M] All public functions/methods/classes have documentation comments (JSDoc for TypeScript)
applicable_sections.details: concerts.ts has minimal JSDoc. Tests do not enforce documentation, but naming is self-explanatory. Document requirement in output.

applicable: [C] No PII appears in any log statement at any level
[C] No PII is included in exception messages that could reach logs
applicable_sections.details: CRITICAL per MEMORY.md session 2026-03-31: Test fixtures MUST use anonymized data (test-user-1@test.example.com, NOT real emails). Privacy flags (hideLocationPublic, hideCostPublic, isPublic) MUST be tested.

critical_requirements: All 25+ exported functions from concerts.ts MUST be covered by unit tests (achieve ≥80% line coverage)
Prisma client MUST be completely mocked; no real database calls in tests
All test fixtures MUST use anonymized data: test-user-1@test.example.com, test-admin@test.example.com, mock UUIDs, generic artist names
External APIs (Last.fm, Photon, MusicBrainz) MUST be mocked; never make real API calls in tests
Privacy flags (hideLocationPublic, hideCostPublic, isPublic) MUST be explicitly tested with dedicated test cases
NO PII must appear in test logs or error messages: log only IDs and operation names, never names/emails/locations
Error handling: ConcertAlreadyExistsError MUST be tested on duplicate detection
Test naming MUST follow pattern: test_{function}_{condition}_{expected} (e.g., test_getConcertsPaginated_with_cursor_returns_correct_page)
high_requirements: Happy path tests for all 25+ functions (basic success scenario with valid inputs)
Edge case tests: null/undefined handling, empty results, boundary values (limit=1, limit=100), date edge cases (today, far future)
Filter validation tests: userId, username, bandSlug, city, year parameters must be validated and safely passed to Prisma
Pagination cursor tests: bidirectional pagination (forward/backward), cursor parameter type checking, invalid cursor handling
Statistics caching tests: verify unstable_cache behavior, test cache invalidation scenarios
Spending aggregation tests: getConcertStatistics, getUserTotalSpent with various cost data
Duplicate detection tests: findMatchingConcert logic with coordinate tolerance (~100m)
JSDoc comments on test file itself documenting test structure and mocking patterns
Tests MUST be independent: no beforeAll state shared between tests; each test sets up its own mocks
Coverage report: run with --coverage flag to verify 80%+ line + branch coverage

setup.mocking_approach: vi.mock() for Prisma client, vi.stubEnv() for env vars
setup.jsdom_not_needed: concerts.ts is pure business logic, no DOM access

mocks.prisma_client: vi.mock('@/lib/prisma', () => ({ prisma: { concert: {}, userConcert: {}, concertBand: {}, band: {}, festival: {} } }))

integration_tests.description: Optional: After unit test suite passes, add integration tests for API routes that call concerts.ts functions
integration_tests.note: Can be deferred to follow-up task if time-boxed. Unit tests are primary focus.

e2e_tests.description: Out of scope for this task. E2E testing handled separately in UI layer.
e2e_tests.note: Unit tests provide sufficient coverage for business logic.

naming_requirements.reasoning: Matches existing Vitest pattern from prior session (helpers.test.ts, featureFlags.test.ts)

naming_requirements.convention: camelCase, descriptive, maps to business behavior
naming_requirements.reasoning: TypeScript/Vitest standard; immediately readable in test runner output

naming_requirements.convention: camelCase, descriptive, no _mock suffix (vi.mock() returns named exports)
naming_requirements.reasoning: Clarity for code reviewers; matches Jest/Vitest idiom
Obvious these are test fixtures, not production data

documentation_requirements.file_docstring: REQUIRED: Block comment at top of test file explaining structure, GDPR compliance, and mock patterns
documentation_requirements.example:
/**
 * Unit tests for src/lib/concerts.ts
 * 
 * Tests cover 25+ exported functions including CRUD, pagination, filtering, statistics.
 * All Prisma calls are mocked; no database access during tests.
 * 
 * GDPR Compliance:
 * - Test fixtures use anonymized data (test-user-1@test.example.com, mock UUIDs)
 * - Privacy flags (hideLocationPublic, hideCostPublic, isPublic) explicitly tested
 * - No PII in logs or error messages
 * 
 * Mocking Strategy:
 * - Prisma client: vi.mock() singleton returned in each test
 * - External APIs: getGeocodingData, cityToSlug stubbed with vi.fn()
 * - Each test sets up its own mock return values; no shared state
 */

documentation_requirements.location: Each test function (via JSDoc comment above describe/it block)
documentation_requirements.requirement: Optional but recommended: brief JSDoc explaining what is tested and why
documentation_requirements.example: /** Happy path: valid concert data creates record, returns TransformedConcert with id. */

documentation_requirements.requirement: Group tests by category (e.g., 'CRUD Operations', 'Pagination', 'Duplicate Detection')

documentation_requirements.update_required: YES — Add section on concerts.ts test patterns, mocking Prisma, GDPR compliance checklist
documentation_requirements.section_content:
### Testing src/lib/concerts.ts

**What to test:**
- CRUD: createConcert, updateConcert, deleteConcert, getConcertById
- Pagination: getConcertsPaginated (cursor-based, bidirectional)
- Filtering: by userId, bandSlug, city, year
- Statistics: aggregations, caching behavior
- Duplicates: findMatchingConcert with coordinate tolerance
- Edge cases: null handling, empty results, boundary values

**Mocking Prisma:**
```typescript
vi.mock('@/lib/prisma', () => ({
  prisma: {
    concert: { create: vi.fn(), update: vi.fn(), findMany: vi.fn() },
    userConcert: { findMany: vi.fn(), delete: vi.fn() },
    concertBand: { findMany: vi.fn() }
  }
}))
```

**GDPR Compliance Checklist (mandatory):**
- [ ] Test fixtures anonymized (test-user-1@test.example.com, not real emails)
- [ ] hideLocationPublic flag tested (location fields nullified when true)
- [ ] hideCostPublic flag tested (cost fields nullified when true)
- [ ] isPublic flag tested (profile visibility controlled)
- [ ] No PII in logs: log only IDs, never names/emails/locations
- [ ] No real API keys in test environment
- [ ] All external APIs mocked (Last.fm, Photon, MusicBrainz)

security_requirements.concern: Input validation on filter parameters (userId, username, bandSlug, city, year)
security_requirements.requirement: Test that getConcertsPaginated validates limit (1-100), rejects malformed input

security_requirements.concern: Cursor parameter integrity (should be valid UUID)
security_requirements.requirement: Test that invalid cursor (non-UUID string, null, undefined) returns safe response (empty array)

security_requirements.concern: Privacy flags in public profile queries
security_requirements.requirement: GDPR-critical: hideLocationPublic and hideCostPublic flags MUST be tested. Response must nullify sensitive fields.
Verify error is thrown on duplicate detection and NOT exposed as raw exception to client

security_requirements.concern: No PII leakage in test logs
security_requirements.requirement: Ensure test output logs only IDs (UUIDs), operation names, never user emails or personal details
security_requirements.test_scenario: All test fixtures use test-user-1@test.example.com, mock UUIDs, generic artist names

observability_requirements.requirement: Tests do NOT enforce logging (logging is separate implementation task), but document as HIGH gap
observability_requirements.recommendation: Follow-up task: Add structlog-style logging to entry/exit of public functions (createConcert, updateConcert, deleteConcert, getConcertsPaginated, getConcertStatistics)
expected_log_fields: concertId or userId (entity ID only, never names)

observability_requirements.requirement: Tests MUST NOT log PII. Verify test fixtures use anonymized identifiers.

critical_findings.detail: Tests MUST explicitly verify hideLocationPublic, hideCostPublic, isPublic flags. When hideLocationPublic=true, latitude/longitude must be nullified in response. When hideCostPublic=true, cost field must be nullified.
critical_findings.reference: MEMORY.md session 2026-03-31: 'Privacy flag testing is CRITICAL'

critical_findings.detail: All test fixtures MUST use fake/anonymized data. NO real email addresses, NO real names, NO real photos.

fixtures_required.user_id: mock-uuid-1, mock-uuid-2 (use crypto.randomUUID() or hardcoded for reproducibility)
fixtures_required.band_name: Test Artist One, Test Artist Two (generic, not real artists)
fixtures_required.image_urls: null (never reference real photos or URLs)

critical_findings.reference: MEMORY.md session 2026-03-31: 'Test fixtures MUST use anonymized data'

critical_findings.detail: Test logs must never include: full request bodies, passwords, tokens, IBAN, email addresses, names, dates of birth. Log only: correlation_id, entity IDs (UUIDs), operation names.
Optional: Test UserConcert cascade delete to verify RTBF mechanism works (delete user → deletes all their UserConcert records).

gdpr_compliance.audit_trail: No new audit requirements. Concert model already has createdById/updatedById fields.
gdpr_compliance.data_residency: No external APIs send PII. All data stays in PostgreSQL (EU-region).

risks.description: Coverage target 80% means 20% of code paths untested. High-risk functions not covered may have latent bugs.
risks.mitigation: Prioritize coverage on: CRUD, pagination, duplicate detection, statistics. Accept lower coverage on utility functions (getStartOfToday, parseSupportingActIds).

risks.description: Prisma mock complexity. concerts.ts chains Prisma queries (findMany → select → transform). Mock setup must handle nested object structures.
risks.mitigation: Build mock helpers to generate realistic Prisma response shapes. Example: createMockConcert(id, date, lat, lon) returns full PrismaConcert with related bands.

risks.description: No observability in concerts.ts. Tests cannot verify logging behavior (no logs currently implemented).
risks.mitigation: Document logging gap as HIGH priority follow-up task. Tests focus on business logic only. Separate task: add structlog-style logging per quality-engineer.md standard.

risks.description: getEffectiveBandsForForm() logic unclear from code review. Function transforms support acts + headliner. Test edge cases carefully.
risks.mitigation: Add dedicated test scenarios for support act parsing: null, empty array, malformed items, duplicate sortOrder values.

risks.description: GDPR compliance gap: Concert model has latitude/longitude (location data — Category 2 PII). Tests MUST verify privacy flags work.
risks.mitigation: 2 dedicated tests for hideLocationPublic and hideCostPublic flags. Mark tests with gdpr_flag=true in test metadata.

risks.description: Test data isolation: If tests share mock instances between test cases, state bleeding will cause flaky tests.
risks.mitigation: Each test calls vi.resetAllMocks() or beforeEach(() => vi.clearAllMocks()). Verify Vitest setup.ts includes global mock reset.

files_to_create.description: Comprehensive unit test suite for concerts.ts (25+ tests, 80% coverage target)
Mock factory functions for creating realistic test data (mockConcert, mockUserConcert, mockBand, etc.)

sections_to_add: ### Testing src/lib/concerts.ts — Test patterns, mocking Prisma, GDPR compliance

files_to_update.change_required: NO — scripts already exist from prior Vitest setup (test, test:watch, test:coverage)

adoption_notes.vitest_readiness: READY — Vitest framework and configuration already in place from prior session (2026-03-31)
adoption_notes.gdpr_guidelines: READY — TESTING.md already documents GDPR compliance requirements from prior session
adoption_notes.precedent: Build on 4 existing example test files (helpers.test.ts, featureFlags.test.ts, lastfm.test.ts, ConcertCard.test.tsx) with proven patterns
adoption_notes.team_readiness: Vitest API compatible with Jest; team should be familiar with basic testing patterns from examples

summary_for_implementation: Create __tests__/lib/concerts.test.ts with 25-35 test cases covering CRUD, pagination, filtering, statistics, error handling, and GDPR privacy flags. Use vi.mock() for Prisma client and external APIs. Target 80% line coverage. Follow existing test naming conventions. Mark privacy flag tests with gdpr_flag=true. Use anonymized fixtures (test-user-1@test.example.com, mock UUIDs, generic artist names). Update TESTING.md with concerts.ts test patterns and GDPR compliance checklist. No logging tests required (logging is separate follow-up task).
